# Why MCP Saves 90% of Your Manual AI Work | Model Context Protocol Explained
URL: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xBcSLxpIlr0
Channel: Grace Leung
Published:  | Duration: 00:14:57 | Views: 59995

---

Do you know what makes a true difference
between a powerful AI agent's AI webflow
versus a simple AI chat bot that is the
ability to access and use external tools?
While most LLM today are decided to
handle back and forth conversations, true
AI agents can connect to your calendar
database, email, any service or tools
your business use, and perform the tasks
on the fly and the technology making
this possible and scalable at the same
time is MCP model context protocol.
So in this video I explain
everything you need to know about
MCP, including practical demos.
So you know why MCP is a big
piece for building powerful
AI workflows or AI agents.
Let's go.
So what is MCP?
By definition, MCP is an open
source protocol that standardize how
applications provide context to LLM.
And let me explain this.
Before MCP, connecting AI to your
business tools or system, it's
like trying to lead a team where
everyone speaks a different language.
Imagine your AI needs to
get data from Salesforce.
It has to learn to speak Salesforce and
then for it to understand your company
Google Analytics, your AI needs to
learn a completely different language.
Each connection is like a custom
one-off translation project.
You or your team has to act
as an individual translator.
Teaching the AI how to communicate
with each tool, one by one.
And this is using what we call
the traditional API method.
Of course this method works 100%, but the
biggest problem is because AI is changing
so fast, this method simply doesn't scale.
When the AI model changes or a new
AI model rolls out, imagine you
have 10 different tools you are
managing 10 separate conversation.
So that's why the solution we
have MCP, think of it like a
universal translator for the AI.
Instead of AI learning 10 different
languages, it only needs to
learn one, the language of MCP.
***REMOVE***??? And so when service provider like
Salesforce, slack, also adopt
MCP, they all speak the same
standard language and save much of
your custom integration efforts.

The simplified way to understand

the two main components,
The MCP server and the

We have MCP servers.

So when the client says, I need to

# 7 Skills That Make You Irreplaceable (When AI’s Here)
URL: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jCrZ1b8GMDI
Channel: Grace Leung
Published:  | Duration: 00:11:30 | Views: 12389

---

Millions of jobs will disappear faster
than most people realize, and most skills
might become irrelevant when AI is here.
People hate to hear this.
I understand that there is
always some mixed emotion, but
AI is impacting every industry.
Even for myself, I'm constantly re

One third of our life is spent at work.

as a person who has lost my job and is
building everything from the ground up
again, there is so much career advice I

so first do not mix up

on any employer but yourself.

we all tied our self value with

to identify the opportunity gaps.

you must have heard of them before.

will always generate better results.
Everyone will tell you that you
must assign a role in the prompt.
Act as an expert consultant, act as
an experienced marketing professional.
I'm not saying that assigning a role is
not useful, but role prompting might not
be always be as effective as you think.
And from my experience, especially when
prompting using those more advanced
models like GPT 4 model 3.5 Sonnet,
the difference is not quite noticeable.
A study about the effectiveness
of role prompting also shows
that assigning roles will not
consistently improve the response.
Indeed, using a 2-shot chain of
thought prompting is even better.
And more interesting, in experiments
to ask AI to act like an idiot
versus a genius, the idiot
prompt actually outperformed.
Another research also reveals that
the role prompting performance are
often unpredictable and use them
when there is a clear and logical
alignment between the task and the role
that is to say, you might not
need a role in every prompt.
Generally for now, I see assigning roles
works better for tasks that require more

Also, use concrete,

eat healthy I know all all these sounds

# Best Ways to Use Gemini 2.0 (over ChatGPT & Perplexity)!
URL: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kN93lrS1nfw
Channel: Grace Leung
Published:  | Duration: 00:16:06 | Views: 81433

---

Google is making a lot of serious moves
in AI, and the recent launch of Gemini 2.0
just shocked the world.
You know, at first, I was skeptical.
But honestly speaking, after
trying it, this is impressive.
So, in this video, I will share what
makes Gemini 2 unique, its limitations,
and some of the best ways to use it over
ChatGPT or other AI tools like Perplexity.
Let's go.
What makes Gemini 2 truly
unique compared to other AIs,
it has multi modal capability.
Unlike ChatGPT, which became multi
modal in the middle, Gemini has always
been multi modal since the beginning.
So you will find the experience
is more native and integrated.
Not just enhanced multi modal capability
in Gemini 2 to handle text, audio,
video much better at the same time.
But the multi modal live API
just takes it to the next level.
You can now interact with Gemini
in real time streaming, like
video, webcam, or screen sharing.
Even for ChatGPT for now, you can only
do it through the mobile app version, and
it is a completely different experience.
Second, it is the multilingual
native audio output, which I found
people seldom talks about, but this
is definitely one of the biggest
strength of Gemini in natural language
processing and native audio output.
Remember why everyone loves
NotebookLM audio?
The control over tone, style, accents,
pacing, emotions are all superior.
Now in Gemini 2, it even
supports generating native
audio in multiple languages.
Although these upgrades are
not yet available in the
standard Gemini Advanced yet,
for now, you can test it
through the Google AI Studio
until they are fully roll out.
But keep in mind, everything you
upload in the free version of AI
Studio may be used for model training.
So I recommend just playing
with the publicly available data
and not some sensitive data.
Of course, Gemini 2 is not perfect
and it's still experimental
under Gemini advanced.
For now, I find the image generation
is still limited, and Google
has been so cautious in handling
anything related to people.
Also, even in AI studio with less
filtering, Gemini may experience
errors in more sophisticated data
analysis, may give false information
with its real time interactive mode.
So for now, personally, I do not
recommend using it for complex
tasks involving large datasets.
Now, the best use cases, with its
unique strength in language and multi
modal capability, instead of just
asking it to do tasks like static
landing page review, Data analysis
that are better suited for text input.
Here are my best use cases.
The first one is user experience analysis.
Yes, Gemini 2 can see your screen,
but what makes this powerful is to ask
it to analyze the entire process in
real time,
because this is something that can't be
done so easily through the text input
and it's super useful to identify
friction points and suggest
improvement for the user journey flow.
Let's say I have this website
and I will ask Gemini how to
improve the checkout flow.
As a good practice, I always
turn on grounding so it can
search for web data when needed.
Now click share your screen
and select the right page.
I'm going to walk through
the entire checkout process
on my website step by step.
So as I navigate each page, please help

So why we need to use both

Perplexity can do web
searches and find sources.

ask a question, it will trigger a new
search and use different sets of sources.
And even if you upload your own
knowledge base to the Perplexity
Space, it may still use some pre
trained data to answer your question.
And so this is why NotebookLM is
useful, because it will only give
you answers based on your sources,
and the source reference will not
change unless you import new source.
And so it's better to organize your
notes, insights with its closed database.

different research projects.

you can go back to Perplexity to

# How to Be More DISCIPLINED - 5 Tips to Stop Procrastinating
URL: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=L5zRZwExaf4
Channel: Grace Leung
Published:  | Duration: 00:10:30 | Views: 1385

---

in the past I've tried to make lots of
life changing habits tases and few of
them stake and I would give myself
excuses so I can escape that discomfort
immediately like consistently writing
video script for my YouTube channel is
so hard and I almost gave up self-
discipline is about having the mental
power to choose the boring thing even
you feel like picking the comfortable
option we don't have to be smarter than
the rest we have to be more disciplined
than the rest if we look at successful
people we often think they are talented
intelligent and that drive their success
but if we take a closer look it's often
that they are highly disciplined and
during those boring things consistently
regard regardless of feeling if you want
to live a life that you want and
accomplish more you need to be highly
self-discipline so in this video I'll
share the five tips that have helped me
to build the self-discipline and develop
that inner strength the first and the
biggest one is to know your burning
desire and give it a purpose to things
that you feel like you don't want to do
intense burning desire is the
motivational force that enable you to
overcome any obstacle and Achieve almost
any goal feel it's difficult to build
self-discipline is because often times
we only focus on the hard work that we
need to put in at this very moment but
not the future and so you need to know
why you're doing this because you will
need to constantly remind yourself of
your vision in order to push through all
the Temptations to give up for me my
burning design now is so simple building
my own dream but not adds live a life
that aligns with my values and never
trade my time so easily just for money
or a fancy job title for example if you
have a burning desire or set a vision
for yourself to have a stronger
healthier body one year later that you
become unrecognizable then spending 30
minutes daily doing a gym has a great
meaning to your long-term Vision even if
you feel like it's boring and it's hard
as I also mentioned in my previous video
The Daily tiny gains that you get
through self discipline will have a
compound impact on your life so I would
like you to visualize your future
visualize the life that you want it
doesn't have to be 10 years later but
imagine one year later where will you be
so you will tell yourself it's not
because you have to do it it because you
choose to do that today no matter what
and become the best version of yourself
tomorrow so what is really driving here
is how you can delay that gratification
so resisting immediate Temptation or
rewards for your longterm term goals and
this is what highly disciplined people
can do so well and the coming tips that
I'm going to share also have a big part

I was one of those who was impacted by the massive

want to share some of the truth that I came to
realize and that has led me to make that decision 
not to go back to 95 caveat this is not a video
encouraging you to quit your day job on 95 instead 
I just want to share my own experience my insight
and hopefully inspire you to rethink your approach

whether you should quit or not the reasons why 
people thinking quitting 95 is because they want
to pursue Freedom ultimately we all want freedom 
but at the same time people are feeling stuck is

we all need safety but what I came to realize is 
that nothing called real security it just doesn't

as a full-time employee or do you feel safe just

working 95 you can still being let go by your

your own you can still face a lot of challenges
is for a business everything can happens overnight

you're creating your own security and it shouldn't 
depends on your day job or your employer you

because I understand no matter what happened I

# Calculate Digital Marketing Budget (For Beginners)
URL: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=t2s5H98Pi7k
Channel: Grace Leung
Published:  | Duration: 00:13:11 | Views: 2699

---

hey guys welcome back to my channel
Grace here today I will teach you a
simple but effective way of calculating
your digital marketing budget for your
campaigns well there's so many different
ways of calculating your digital
marketing budget but this is by far one
of my favorite methods because it's
scalable completely data different and
so can give you more realistic budget
forecast budget planning is the crucial
first step to ensure your campaign
success because it allows you to align
your digital marketing efforts to your
business goal and form a baseline to
keep track of your campaign performance
and make strategic optimization but
before I start I appreciate hitting the
Subscribe button now so that you won't
miss any videos from my channel in the
future now let's Dive Right
In Step number one identify your
campaign Target kvi for any kinds of
business whether it's B2B b2c you should
have a clear business goal and it will
be translated to Target kpi for your
marketing campaigns and therefore it's
so important to first set a measurable
kpi for your campaigns in order to
support that goal maybe it's driving x
amount of sales dollars maybe it's
driving x amount of new customers or
maybe it's driving x amount of brand
impressions for most of the campaign
revenue is usually the Target kbii and
in case your campaign is driving new
customers I still recommend you to put a
revenue Target that is equivalent to new
customer Target this will help you to
quantify your marketing investment and
I'll show you why in the next minute for
this video I prepared template for you
to follow the process step by step and
you can download it from the description
below now open the template write down
your goal and put an exact figure for
that kpi metric as an example let's use
$20,000 as our campaign Target kpi
typing
$20,000 step number two determine the
average transaction value now you have
the Reven Target the next thing you're
going to know is the average transaction
value also known as ATV ATV revers to
the average amount your customer are
spending on an each purchase and in the
BTC business it refer exactly what it is
that is the average transaction value
while in a B2B business it refers to the
average deal size to calculate the
average transaction value or the average
deal size you need to grab the
historical total revenue and number of
transactions for the past 6 months and
divided the total revenue by the number
of orders or deals to get their ATV for
the time period now let's type 100 as an
example
step number three determine the
conversion Target now you have the
refence target you also have the aage
transaction value then you can determine
the conversion Target for your marketing
campaigns in short the conversion Target
is the number of conversions you aim to
get in order to hit the campaign kpr
Target in BTC business as simple as it
directly refers to the number of
transaction or ERS made the formula will
be dividing the Reven Target by the aage
transaction value well in B2B it refers
to the number of leads that you need to
acquire and because leads are converted
to mql SQL and opportunities before they
become a Clos to deal you still need to
know the lead to deal converter r that
is how many leads are eventually
converted to close the deals so that you
can get your lead Target usually you can
grab the number of closer deals and
number of leads from your CM platform
and you can get the commercial rate by
dividing the number of closer deal by
the number of leads for the time period
And if you really don't know you can do
some research and get your industry
Benchmark so in a case of B2B divided
your Revenue Target by the aage deal
size to get your target number of deals
then divided it by the lead to deal
conversion rate we mentioned earlier to
get your target number of leads for your
budget plan so continue our calculation
assume it's a BTC business divide the
revenue Target by the every transaction
value which gives you 200 transactions
step number four identify your digital
Market channels and set a conversion
Target for each Channel now you're ready
to kick off your campaign because you
have a clear conversions or lead Target
the next thing you're going to design
your channel strategy that is which
digital marketing channels you're going
to use for your campaign every business
is different for some online Brands
perhaps social media or pay search alone
can drive over 50 percentage of its
Revenue but it may not apply to your
case so go to your web analytics store
and find out the approximate percentage
of total transaction or lease each
channel is contributing in the past 6
months and set a conversion time Target
percentage for designed channels for
each channel the conversion or lead
Target will be multiplying the total
conversions or lead Target by your
target percentage now back to our
template for example I know the
percentage number from the historical
data for pay search like Google ass and
Bank ass are driving 40% of total
transactions so I will set a Target here
and do the rest of the channels that
we're going to use so they will all add
up to 100% no you should also add in
organic channels like like search or
emails if they were also contributing to
the company goals in the past so you can
have a holistic view on your digital
marketing plan you will notice I I've
also add display but since display is
mainly used for Branding its
contribution to the revenue is really
low but still we want to input a Target
here perhaps 2% and once you type in the
Target percentage the target conversion
for that channel will be automatically
calculated based on the percentage of
that Target important to keep in mind
that the historical data is only to give
you a baseline of what Target you should
set for that channel but it doesn't mean
you should use the same percentage as
the Target also if you're planning to
add in any new channels without any
historical data try not to set a really
high percentage Target so that to make
your budget plan more realistic if you
really don't have any historical data to
reference try to do a bit of research to
understand what are the key channels
your audience is using or you can go to
similar web that is a competitive
intelligence platform and type in your
competitor for your target market to get
an understanding of the key channels
that are using step number five
determine the conversion rate for each
Channel a conversion rate is the
percentage of users who have taken a
designed action such as making a
purchase filling out a form for an ebook
so understanding the conversion rate for
each channel is super important for more
effective budgeting because then you can
have a sense of how effective of that
channel Inver buing a traffic to
purchase or to lead so that you can
estimate how much you going to spend for
that channel in BTC it is calculated as
the number of transaction divided by the
number of trave in the B2B it is
calculated as the number of leads
divided by the number of trave again go
to your website analytics or CLM
platform to grab your past historical
data and determine your conversion rate
for that channel if you don't have any
idea try to research for your industry
Benchmark step number six calculate the
target number of traffic for each
Channel all right we have the
approximate number of conversion Target
for each Channel we also have the
conversion rate now we're going to
calculate the number of traic that
requireed for each channel in order to
hit our total conversion or leas Target
to calculate this for each Channel use
this formula divide the conversion or
least Target by the conversion rate for
that channel for example for p channel
the conversion Target is 90 and you know
the conversion rate for this channel is
4% you can now derive the target traffic
needed from this channel in order to hit
this target which is around 2,000 then
calculate for the rest of the
channel step number seven determine the
average cost per click for each Channel
we've getting almost everything we need
we're so close now we still need the
final piece that is the average cost per
click for your PID channels also known
as CPC every CPC is calculated by
dividing the total cost by the total
number of clicks for the time period you
can simply grab this number from the app
platforms for example go to Google ads
and you can check the average cost per
click for your past campaigns if you
don't have any historical data you can
also research different CPC benchmarks
for industry to get the closest estimate
now as an example let's put $2 for pay
search $1 for social channels and
$0.5 for display for organic channels
there will be no average cost per click
so let's just put in zero step number
eight calculate the Media budget for
each Channel and sum them up the final
step you will get the media budget
required for each Channel by using this
formula multiply the target number of
traffic by the aage CPC for that Channel
Once you calculate the budget for each
Channel you can now sum them up to get
the Total Media budget that you need for
example for this campaign to drive
$20,000 sales with an ATV of 100 we need
around
$9,000 of Media budget however to
calculate the total digital marketing
budget that you need you still need to
consider any cost other than your Media
budget such as cost of buying stock
photos or doing ad creatives so add them
to the Media budget and you can get the
final total digital marketing budget for
your campaign for example on top of the
Media budget we also need $500 to do add
creatives then the total digital
marketing budget will be adding up these
two figures that is
$110,000 now a very important thing is
double check track the rowes that is
return on asban for a campaign budget
rowes is a metric used to measure the
effectiveness of your campaign in
generating Revenue to calculate the
rowes divide the revenue generated by
the total digital marketing cost of the
campaign in this example that is around
two if you find the RAS is less than one
then it means you have made a budget
forecast that yields a negative return
because the total cost is bigger than
the revenue in most cases if your
campaign is driving Revenue the rowes
should be at least between 1.5 to three
or even bigger now putting the
percentage of Total Media budget for
each Channel if you find for any channel
the percentage of conversions is less
than the percentage of total budget
meaning that channel is causing more
budget but not effectively translating
into conversions and so revenue and so
you may wish to allocate less budget for
that channel for this example you can
see social channels are not that
effective for the business so you can
allocate less budget for other
performing channels or add some more
channels to diversify the budget now you
understand the basic idea and strategy
for calculating your digital marketing
campaign budget and you will find this
method highly scalable because you can
add other channels as you need and here
is a few more bonus tips for you first
if you're also doing other non
payperclick channnels such as third
party sponsorship or affiliate Channels
with a fixed budget you can do the other
way around by first putting your budget
for that Channel and then input the
conversion Target from your historical
data or you can do some research on the
industry Benchmark about the cost per
transaction or cost belete information
for that channel so that you can see how
that channel will help you to hit the
final conversion Target with that cost
second if you're in B2B and have
sufficient historical data about the
cost Bel lead information you can
actually skip step number five to seven
and directly calculate the budget for
each Channel by multiplying the cost Bel
information with the least t Target
third identify whether your campaign is
mainly Target your existing customers or
new customers because it will impact on
your average cost per click or
conversion rate data for example for new
customers the cost per click or cost per
lead is usually higher because they're
more expensive to acquire than existing
customers so you definitely need to
consider that when calculating your
budget fourth consider any seasonality
factors that will impact on your
conversion rate and cost per click
forecast try to analyze the same period
of data from previous year and get a
sense of how the conversion rate and
cost per click will fluctuate across the
year for example for retailers the
holiday season is usually have a higher
cost per click because every Advertiser
will do promotion during that period
lastly which is my favorite is to do
your budget under different scenario
optimistic pessimistic and realistic
what would your budget looks like under
the worst case scenario where conversion
rate is low CPC is high or under an
optimistic scenario where every met are
doing good this way you can get a sense
of budget ranging from the lowest to the
highest that cover the worst to the best
case scenario so you can estimate what
should be the B there's so many ways of
calculating your digital marketing
budget and the more you do this exercise
the more you realize you need historical

examples, methods, evidence, like
this post, I asked it to propose three
areas where I can add concrete details.

Fourth, break the pattern.
AI always default to a certain writing

a question, not a statement.

ability to scale with ai.
Most people use one AI for everything.

combining tools strategically.
So each handles what it does best.
And here are three of my favorite
workflow patterns to get you started.
Pattern number one, research
to polished deliverables.
For example, I can just do focus
search on perplexity, our specialized
AI search engines, and grab a list

note taking app by Google to synthesize
and give you grounded insights.
Then we can generate different punished
content, deliverables like slide decks,
infographics, strategy reports, even
video overviews, flashcard mind maps.
And the best thing is they
are all backed by research.
Pattern number two, turn reports
or documents into slide decks.
For example, we can start with
ChatGPT Agent Mode, which is
a unique features of ChatGPT.
Let it do an in-depth website audit
by browsing, taking actions, and
prepare a comprehensive audit report.
And then let Gemini turn this audit

is ready to be export to Google slide.
So you are leveraging the
strength of both AI tools.
Pattern number three, turn reports or
documents into beautiful dashboard.
So like here we can do Perplexity
deep research on certain topics, and
then we can load this entire report
into Gemini and one, click it into a
beautiful dashboard using the built-in
visualization creation feature.
So within 10 minutes we have
transformed a lengthy report of a
complex process into a visual story.
And this is what system
thinking looks like with AI.
You are not picking the
best tool, the best model.
You are building a system where each
tool handles what it excels and they
work together to compound your outputs.
Right now you can make AI work for you
one task at a time, but what happens
when you need the same workflows running
10 times a week, or a task that takes
five steps across two different tools?
That's why the next logical
stage, AI automation and agents,
basically there are three levels,
starting with the easiest one.
Level one, scheduled task
and custom AI assistant.
This is using automation feature
built-in from ChatGPT Gemini Perplexity.

search for the latest news from
the past week on specific topics.

seconds and now you will start
receiving email notification.
Then set specialized AI assistant
a custom GPT, Gemini Gem with
predefined workflow details,
like a GEM that generates
presentation outlines automatically
from long documents or reports.
Even a simple AI automation like
this will save you huge amount
of time doing recurring tasks.
Level two simple AI automation.
AI automation follows a fixed path
to achieve your goal with a trigger.
So everything is highly predictable.
So when something happen, AI will do
a sequence of actions automatically.
So most no-code automation tools like
Zapier, Make.com will get you started
easily with the visual builder.
Just define your trigger, like an email
arrival, a form submit, and the following
step that it should take on what tool.
And even Google has its
Gemini-powered workflow builder
that is equally intuitive.
So the technical barrier
is lower than you think.
Level three AI agents.
This is where AI works mostly on
your behalf and decide the best
path to make autonomous decision.
So you may need an AI agent when
your task is more complex and require
more intelligent decision making.
So popular no-code AI agent building
platforms like n8n, Lindy AI, GumpLoop,

with defined criteria, draft emails

Do not automate work that shouldn't exist.

For a period of time, I struggled a lot
over worrying about the results, how
others think about me. Even when I
started this channel early on, I have
experienced some difficult times, like
how to handle very harsh judgment from
people. My genuine tension was twisted
into something else. Awarding some
people that makes me feel so down.
You know, in the past, I would think,
"Oh, maybe because I'm not doing good
enough, I'm not meeting others'
expectation. I'm still not qualified
enough. I need to work harder enough. I
need to achieve more. I need to prove
enough cuz everyone else is doing better
than me." And then, I set a very high
standard for myself to ensure I am
qualified. Until I realized all this
time, I wasn't fighting the world. I was
fighting myself. It's not about the
issues, the people, the consequence, the
outcome, the judgment, the thing that
has happened. It's always is about how I
choose to react. The control is always
in my hands. When you find something
truly meaningful in your life, others'
opinions naturally become less
significant. So, in this video, I want
to share with you my own experience, how
my entire world shifted when I stopped
caring less and less about others'
people opinion, and instead caring more
and more about myself. The power of
detachment. Detachment isn't to stop
caring completely for others. We still
cares about others' opinion because we
want to improve. That is part of human
nature. Instead, we experience feelings
without letting them control our
emotions and decision-making. When I
first started this channel, I was so
afraid of seeing new comments because I
couldn't help myself from thinking,
"Perhaps that's another negative
comments." When my channel started to
grow, I start receiving troll comments.
I still remember one of my previous
videos,
um where someone left a comment saying
that woman should stay in a kitchen,
something like that. Or make jokes out

know that's an unpopular opinion, but
the truth is we have to face it. And
that is something we need to learn in

control people. There are people in my

what I'm doing. People who have opinions
on me, people who dislike me. But the

them control our emotions. To be honest,
sometimes I still have heart feeling.
I'm not that perfect human, but it's a
really short one and then I will just

negative things and that is my rule.

insecure and desperate. And the more I'm
feeling insufficient because I always
laying the external response to judge if
I'm making the right decision. And don't
get me wrong, getting external
validation is part of human psychology.
As human beings, we want to feel seen,
feel heard, and feel understood. We need
social connections to feel safe and
belongings. For myself, I also need this
external support, but there is a
difference between building an external
support system as a source of
motivations versus only relying on
external source to drive motivations. If
your motivation is only coming from
external people like your peers, your
friends, your parents, your boss, but
not from within, you're risking your
happiness and you will find yourself end
up being a people-pleaser. You know, I
was so afraid to start my own business
even though I had thought about it for a
long time because I know my parents,
they won't agree. My friends would
suggest me not to because it doesn't
feel safe. They are all for good
intentions. But then, I lost my
confidence and thought that I wasn't
ready and I rather study MBA, crafting
my skills by doing more freelance work,
building things on the side, and
hopefully one day I can convince them
I'm actually ready. Do I have the full
confidence when I finally make the leap?
No, but I definitely gain more
confidence along the way. And now I
learned that the person that I need to
convince the most in the first place is
myself, nobody else. I don't need to
explain to anyone. I don't need to prove
to anyone. Don't need to convince anyone
but myself. It's not about
self-confidence.
This is self-assurance. It's an inner
state that you're forming a deeper sense
of self-worth and self-trust. And my
biggest takeaway is if you want it, the
first step is to take the first step.
Because when you start thinking how
other people react, you become hesitant
and inaction. So, the first step is
always the hardest. Don't let others
influence your actions so easily. I can
tell you amazing thing happens when I
care less about others' opinion but more
about my growth. I'm actually attracting
more opportunities and like minded
people to my own circle. It changed
everything. I met so many people who are
also in the same journey as me and
expand my support system. But remember
how it first started. That is to
convince myself to take the first step.
The next, accept the fact that not every
friend or even work relationship is
positive. I found a truth is there are
different levels of relationships. Some
are good, some are bad. Relationships
are two-way. Both parties must invest
efforts, support to maintain the
balance. Sometimes, even if I have made
an effort trying to build a good
relationship, try to be kind to someone,
try to be friendly to someone, I still
can't change or control how they react
or to expect they would do the same to
me. And my solution has always been
acknowledge that, accept that, and move
on. Acknowledge and accept the fact that
we cannot make this relationship better
for now. Maybe in the future it can be
improved. It is not easy to move on
sometimes because we as human beings, we
have our own feelings. We may still be
disappointed for a period of time, but I
found that the moment when I am not
chasing and just being my best, acting
my best, making my best efforts, I
started to attract the people who share
the same values as me, who also emit the
same energy as me. So, all I wanted to
say is you just need to keep being
yourself and don't try to control
others. Don't think changing yourself or
compromising yourself can earn a good
relationship. It won't. Accept the fact
that some people are not meant to be in
your circle, and that's okay. I hope you
find my sharing helpful. And again, I
know that it's not easy. It also took me
a long time to practice and overcome it.
The happiness of your life depends upon
the quality of your thoughts. Let me
know in the comments how you experience
in stop caring too much. I also have an
other playlist of videos about different
growth topics.
I wish you all doing great. I'll see you
next time.
[Music]
